reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is -1.4% and 20-day momentum is 5.4%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 5.2% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.4%, downside volatility near 2.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 6.9%.
Volume confirmation is 2.6% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is -1.6% versus SPY over 5 days and 1.7% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.82, confidence 0.73, and 0.5 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.82, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.5 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.68, average source quality 0.79, confidence 0.73.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.02).
Overall model score is 0.070 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Tesla Hikes Model Y Prices as U.S. Blockade of Iran Intensifies and LIRR Strike Paralyzes New York
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct company action: first Model Y price hike in ~2 years (up to ~$1k). That should lift ASPs/margins modestly and is likely to be viewed positively in the near term, though demand effects could offset some benefit over a longer horizon.
